message:
fix(twitter): URL-encode query parameters per RFC 3986

Query values containing reserved characters (spaces, &, =, +, etc.) were
being passed unencoded, causing malformed URLs and potential API errors.

- Added QUERY_ENCODE_SET constant defining characters requiring encoding
- Applied utf8_percent_encode() to query values before URL construction
- Uses percent_encoding crate (already a dependency via reqwest)

Also: fcp-conformance test formatting fixes (rustfmt alignment)
- Generified emit_scenario_log to accept any Serialize evidence
- Removed dead_code allow on MODULE constant (was unused)
- Reformatted multi-line assertions for readability

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
